title: Longest Palindromic Substring
description:
## Longest Palindromic Substring

Given a string `s`, return the longest substring of `s` that is a palindrome. A palindrome reads the same forwards and backwards.

If there are multiple palindromic substrings of the same maximum length, return the one that starts earliest.

**Constraints:**
- `1 <= s.length <= 1000`
- `s` consists of only digits and English letters.

examples.explanation: "bab" starts at index 0; "aba" also has length 3 but starts at index 1.
The entire string is a palindrome.

hiddenCases.explanation: No palindrome longer than 1; return first
aca appears twice; first at index 1

solutions.label: Expand Around Center — O(n²)
solutions.explanation: Every palindrome has a center — either a single character (odd length) or a pair of matching characters (even length). Expand outward from each possible center while characters match. Track the longest palindrome found. Since we scan left to right and only update when strictly longer, we naturally return the leftmost one.

solutions.label: DP — O(n²)
solutions.explanation: Build a 2D DP table where `dp[i][j]` is True if `s[i..j]` is a palindrome. Base cases: every single character, every matching pair. For longer substrings: `s[i..j]` is a palindrome if `s[i] == s[j]` and `s[i+1..j-1]` is also a palindrome. Track the start and length of the longest palindromic substring found. Uses O(n²) space vs O(1) for the expand-around-center approach.

hints: Every palindrome has a center: either a single character (odd length) or two matching characters (even length). Try expanding from each possible center.
For each index i, check both odd-length palindromes (centered at i) and even-length palindromes (centered between i and i+1).
